story: Every May 20, people around the world celebrate World Bee Day by learning about these amazing little workers! story: Baseball star David Wells threw his first pitch into the world on May 20, 1963. Born in Torrance, California, Wells grew up to become a legendary pitcher. title: A School Paid for by the People
story: Imagine a town where everyone chips in to build a school! That's exactly what happened in Dorchester, Massachusetts in 1639. The townspeople decided to use their tax money to create the very first public school in America. This clever idea meant all children could learn, not just those from wealthy families. title: The Pen That Changed Writing Forever
story: Ink-stained fingers became a thing of the past in 1830. On May 20 that year, Douglass Hyde received the first US patent for a fountain pen. This clever invention stored ink inside, allowing for smooth writing without constant dipping. Hyde's design paved the way for portable, mess-free writing tools we still use today.

title: Flames Threaten York's Ancient Cathedral
story: Smoke billowed from York Minster as flames engulfed the historic cathedral on May 20, 1840. The fire, which started in the roof, threatened to destroy centuries of history.
